notifPrefs.smsNotifsDesc: Get text messages for critical updates

notifPrefs.documentDesc: When documents are uploaded or reviewed

help.contactAdmin: Contact your project administrator or reach out to support.

articles.appOverviewContent:
AccuDone helps you manage construction projects from start to finish. The dashboard gives you a bird's-eye view of all your projects, and you can drill into individual projects to manage phases, documents, photos, and team members.

Key areas:
• Dashboard — Overview of all projects and quick stats
• Projects — Create and manage individual projects
• Timeline — Visual Gantt chart of project phases
• Directory — Manage your staff/contractor contacts
• Notifications — Stay updated on project activity
• Settings — Customize theme, manage templates
articles.firstProjectContent:
To create a project:
1. Go to Projects from the sidebar
2. Click 'New Project' in the top right
3. Fill in the project name, address, and estimated completion date
4. Optionally set a budget
5. Click 'Create Project'

Once created, you'll be taken to the project overview page where you can add phases, invite team members, and start tracking progress.
articles.rolesContent:
AccuDone has five user roles:

• Admin — Full access to everything including settings, user management, and all projects
• Project Manager — Can create/edit projects, manage phases, approve documents, and invite members
• Contractor — Access to assigned phases via the Contractor Portal, can upload photos/documents and request reviews
• Stakeholder — Read-only access to project overviews and reports
• Viewer — Basic read-only access

Role determines what actions you can take and what menu items you see.
articles.projectOverviewContent:
The project overview shows:
• Budget summary (total, spent, remaining)
• Progress bar showing phase completion percentage
• Quick stats (active phases, reviews pending, overdue items)
• All phases with status indicators
• Team members and recent activity

Click on any phase to see its detailed view, or use the Timeline link to see the Gantt chart.
articles.budgetTrackingContent:
Set a project budget when creating or editing a project. Each phase can have an estimated cost and actual cost.

The Budget section on the project page shows:
• Phase-by-phase cost breakdown
• Variance (difference between estimated and actual)
• Color-coded warnings when over budget

Admins and PMs can edit phase costs by clicking the edit button in the budget table.
articles.phaseLifecycleContent:
Phases progress through these statuses:

1. Pending — Not yet started
2. In Progress — Active work underway
3. Review Requested — Contractor has requested PM review
4. Under Review — PM is actively reviewing
5. Complete — Phase is finished and approved

Contractors can request reviews from the Contractor Portal. PMs/Admins can change status from the phase detail page.
articles.phaseDependenciesContent:
You can link phases so that one depends on another. This helps with scheduling:

• A dependency means one phase should finish before another starts
• Lag days can be added between dependent phases
• Circular dependencies are automatically prevented
• Dependencies appear on the project timeline

To add a dependency, go to the phase detail page and use the dependencies section.
articles.checklistTemplatesContent:
Admins can create reusable checklist templates in Settings > Checklist Templates. These templates can then be applied to any phase.

To apply a template:
1. Open a phase detail page
2. Look for the Checklist section
3. Click 'Apply Template' and select a template

Checklist items can be checked off by anyone with edit access. When all items are complete, project managers receive a notification.
articles.documentManagementContent:
Upload documents to any phase for record-keeping:

• Supported formats: PDF, images, Word docs, Excel, and more
• Documents can be marked as Pending, Approved, or Rejected by PMs/Admins
• Each document shows who uploaded it and when
• You can delete documents you uploaded

Common document types: permits, inspection reports, contracts, change orders, material specs.
articles.progressPhotosContent:
Take and upload photos to document construction progress:

• Photos are attached to specific phases
• Each photo can have a caption
• GPS tagging with map view for field workers
• PMs can flag photos that need attention
• Photos display in a grid with lightbox view

Regular photo documentation helps with inspections, client updates, and dispute resolution.
articles.invitingTeamContent:
To invite someone to a project:
1. Go to the project overview page
2. Find the Team section
3. Click 'Invite' and enter their email
4. Select their role (PM, Contractor, Stakeholder, Viewer)
5. They'll receive an email invitation

Invitations expire after 7 days. You can resend or revoke pending invitations from the team section.
articles.notifChannelsContent:
AccuDone sends notifications through three channels:

• In-app — Badge on sidebar + notification center page
• Email — Sent via email for important updates
• SMS — Text messages for critical alerts (requires phone number)

You can customize which types of notifications you receive on each channel in Settings > Notification Preferences.

Quiet hours let you pause email/SMS during off-hours while still receiving in-app notifications.
articles.phaseCommentsContent:
Each phase has a comment section for team communication:

• Post comments to discuss progress, issues, or questions
• Comments show user avatar, name, and timestamp
• Delete your own comments (Admins can delete any comment)
• Comments appear in reverse chronological order

Use comments instead of external messaging to keep all project communication in one place.
articles.themeCustomizationContent:
Admins and PMs can customize the app appearance:

• Choose from multiple color themes (Blue, Green, Orange, Purple, Red, Teal)
• Upload a company logo
• Set a company name

Theme changes apply to all users in the organization. Go to Settings > Appearance to customize.
articles.securityAccessContent:
AccuDone uses secure authentication:

• Email-based login with magic links or password
• Google OAuth integration
• Session-based authentication
• Role-based access control

Contact your administrator to change your role or if you're locked out of your account.
articles.changeOrdersContent:
Change orders track scope, budget, and schedule modifications during construction.

To create a change order:
1. Open a project and go to the phase detail page
2. Find the Change Orders section
3. Click "New Change Order"
4. Enter a title, description, and cost impact
5. Submit for approval

Change order statuses:
• Draft — Being prepared, not yet submitted
• Pending — Submitted and awaiting PM/Admin review
• Approved — Accepted; cost automatically added to phase and project budgets
• Rejected — Declined with optional reason

Approved change orders are reflected in the budget section as "Approved COs" and the adjusted budget total updates automatically.
articles.dailyLogsContent:
Daily logs capture day-to-day construction activity for record-keeping and compliance.

Each log entry includes:
• Date of the log entry
• Weather conditions
• Crew size and hours worked
• Work performed description
• Safety incidents or concerns
• Visitor log

To add a daily log:
1. Open the project detail page
2. Navigate to the Daily Logs section
3. Click "Add Daily Log"
4. Fill in the fields and save

Daily logs are visible to Admins, Project Managers, and assigned Contractors. Use them to maintain an accurate project history.
articles.inspectionsContent:
Track quality inspections and code compliance checks for each phase.

To record an inspection:
1. Open a phase detail page
2. Find the Inspections section
3. Click "New Inspection"
4. Enter the inspection type, inspector name, and date
5. Record the result: Pass, Fail, or Conditional
6. Add notes and any required follow-up items

Inspection results:
• Pass — Phase meets all requirements
• Fail — Issues found that must be corrected
• Conditional — Passes with conditions that need to be met

Inspection history is maintained for compliance documentation and audit trails.
articles.materialsContent:
Track materials ordered, delivered, and installed for each phase.

To add a material entry:
1. Open a phase detail page
2. Find the Materials section
3. Click "Add Material"
4. Enter the material name, quantity, unit cost, and supplier
5. Set the initial status

Material statuses:
• Ordered — Purchase order placed
• Delivered — Received on site
• Installed — Incorporated into the build
• Returned — Sent back to supplier

Tracking materials helps monitor supply chain progress and costs.
articles.subcontractorBidsContent:
Manage competitive bids from subcontractors for project phases.

To request bids:
1. Open a phase detail page
2. Find the Subcontractor Bids section
3. Click "Request Bid"
4. Enter the scope description and deadline
5. Invite subcontractors to submit proposals

Bid management:
• Review submitted bids side by side
• Compare costs, timelines, and qualifications
• Accept the winning bid and notify participants
• Rejected bidders receive automatic notifications

Only Admins and Project Managers can manage bids.
articles.offlineModeContent:
AccuDone works even when you lose internet connectivity — perfect for job sites with poor signal.

How it works:
• The app detects when you go offline and shows an indicator in the header
• You can continue making changes (updating phases, adding comments, toggling checklists, etc.)
• Changes are saved to a local queue on your device
• When you reconnect, queued changes automatically sync to the server

The offline indicator shows:
• Number of pending changes waiting to sync
• Sync progress when reconnecting
• A retry button if any changes fail to sync

All 28 mutation actions work offline, including phase updates, comments, checklist toggles, photo flags, document status changes, and more.

Tip: The app caches pages you have visited, so previously loaded project data is available offline.
articles.analyticsDashboardContent:
The analytics dashboard provides visual insights into project performance.

Available widgets:
• Budget overview — Total spend vs. budget across all projects with progress bars
• Phase status distribution — Pie chart showing how phases are distributed across statuses
• Activity timeline — Recent project activity plotted over time
• Project health — At-a-glance health indicators for each active project

Access analytics from the Dashboard page. Widgets update in real time as project data changes.

Admins and Project Managers see analytics for all projects. Contractors see only their assigned project data.
articles.gpsPhotosContent:
Photos can be tagged with GPS coordinates to record exactly where on the job site they were taken.

To use GPS photos:
1. When uploading a photo, allow location access if prompted
2. The GPS coordinates are automatically embedded in the photo metadata
3. Tagged photos appear on an interactive map view in the Photos section

The map view shows:
• Pin markers at each photo location
• Click a pin to see the photo thumbnail and details
• Cluster markers when multiple photos are in the same area

GPS tagging requires location permissions in your browser or device. Indoor photos may have less accurate positioning.
articles.clientPortalContent:
The Client Portal gives project owners and stakeholders a read-only view of their project without needing a full account.

How it works:
• Admins and PMs generate a unique access token for each client
• Share the token link with the client
• Clients can view project progress, budgets, photos, and documents
• No login required — the token provides secure, scoped access

Client portal features:
• Project overview with progress and budget summary
• Phase status and timeline view
• Photo gallery
• Document downloads

Tokens can be revoked at any time from the project settings.
articles.bulkImportContent:
Import multiple records at once using CSV files to save time on data entry.

Supported import types:
• Projects — Create multiple projects from a spreadsheet
• Staff/Contacts — Import your team directory
• Phases — Add phases to a project in bulk

To use bulk import:
1. Download the CSV template from the import dialog
2. Fill in your data following the template columns
3. Upload the completed CSV file
4. Review the preview and confirm the import

The import validates data before processing and reports any errors. Only Admins can perform bulk imports.
articles.twoFactorAuthContent:
Add an extra layer of security to your account with two-factor authentication (2FA).

To enable 2FA:
1. Go to Settings
2. Find the Security section
3. Click "Enable 2FA"
4. Scan the QR code with an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.)
5. Enter the 6-digit code to verify setup

Once enabled, you will need to enter a code from your authenticator app each time you sign in.

If you lose access to your authenticator, contact your Admin to reset 2FA on your account.
articles.apiWebhooksContent:
Integrate AccuDone with external systems using the API and webhooks.

API Keys:
• Generate API keys from Settings > API Keys
• Use keys to authenticate REST API requests
• Keys can be revoked at any time

Webhooks:
• Configure webhook URLs to receive real-time event notifications
• Supported events include: project created, phase updated, document uploaded, inspection completed, and more (16 event types total)
• Webhooks are signed with HMAC for security verification
• Filter which events trigger each webhook

Only Admins can manage API keys and webhook configurations.
articles.budgetForecastingContent:
Budget forecasting helps you predict final project costs based on current spending patterns.

The forecast section on the Budget page shows:
• Projected final cost — Estimated total cost at project completion based on current burn rate
• Cost Performance Index (CPI) — Ratio of budgeted to actual cost; above 1.0 means under budget
• Estimate at Completion (EAC) — Projected total using earned value methodology
• Variance at Completion (VAC) — Difference between budget and projected final cost

Forecasts update automatically as you enter actual costs and complete phases. A green indicator means the project is tracking under budget; red means it is trending over budget.

Use forecasting to make proactive budget decisions before costs spiral.
articles.reportSchedulingContent:
Set up automatic report generation and delivery on a recurring schedule.

To schedule a report:
1. Go to the Reports section
2. Click "Schedule Report"
3. Choose the report type (progress, budget, activity)
4. Select frequency (daily, weekly, monthly)
5. Add email recipients
6. Save the schedule

Scheduled reports are generated automatically and delivered via email. You can pause, edit, or delete schedules at any time.

Only Admins and Project Managers can create scheduled reports.
articles.qbSync: Syncing Data Between QuickBooks and Projects
articles.activityLogContent:
The Activity Log tracks all changes across your projects in one place.

Access it from the sidebar (Admins and Project Managers only). Each entry shows:
• Who made the change
• What action was taken (phase status change, document upload, member update, etc.)
• Which project was affected
• When the change occurred

Filtering:
• Filter by project to see activity on a specific job
• Filter by action type to focus on specific changes
• Pagination for browsing through large histories

Undo (Admins only):
Certain actions can be reversed directly from the log:
• Phase status changes
• Project status changes
• Member removals and role changes
• Checklist item toggles

Hover over a log entry to see the Undo button. Undo creates a new log entry recording the reversal.
articles.profileEditingContent:
Update your personal information from the Settings page.

Editable fields:
• Full Name — Your display name across the app
• Phone Number — Used for SMS notifications
• Company — Your organization name

To update your profile:
1. Go to Settings from the sidebar
2. Edit the fields in the 'My Profile' section
3. Click 'Save Changes'

All profile changes are logged in the Activity Log for admin review. Your email address and role cannot be self-edited — contact an Admin for those changes.
articles.directoryBulkOpsContent:
Manage multiple directory contacts at once with bulk operations.

Bulk Select Mode:
1. Go to the Directory page
2. Click the 'Select' button in the header
3. Click contacts to select them (or use Select All)
4. A floating action bar appears with your options

Available bulk actions:
• Move To — Reassign selected contacts to a different category (Team, Subcontractor, Vendor, Inspector)
• Delete — Remove all selected contacts (requires confirmation)

CSV Export:
Click 'Export CSV' to download your entire directory as a spreadsheet. The export includes name, company, role, type, email, phone, and notes for all contacts.
articles.projectArchiveContent:
Archive completed or inactive projects to keep your dashboard clean.

To archive a project:
1. Open the project detail page
2. Click the 'Archive' button in the header
3. Confirm the archive action

Archived projects are hidden from the main project list and dashboard. They still exist in the database and can be restored at any time.

To restore an archived project:
1. Navigate to the archived project (via direct link or search)
2. Click the 'Restore' button
3. The project returns to active status

Only Admins and Project Managers can archive or restore projects.
articles.insuranceCertificatesContent:
Track insurance certificates for subcontractors and vendors in the Staff Directory.

Click any contact and open the Insurance panel to manage their certificates. You can add certificates for 7 coverage types: General Liability, Workers' Comp, Auto, Umbrella, Professional, Builders Risk, and Other.

Each certificate tracks the carrier, policy number, effective and expiry dates, coverage amount, and optional notes. Status badges show automatically:
• Active (green) — coverage is current
• Expiring Soon (amber) — expires within 30 days
• Expired (red) — coverage has lapsed

You can also designate an Umbrella policy for each contact and toggle an umbrella override when umbrella coverage is confirmed.
articles.complianceExportContent:
Export a Compliance CSV from the Directory to get a full spreadsheet of all contacts with their insurance status.

The CSV includes each contact's name, company, type, email, phone, location, and a summary of their insurance coverage: certificate count, active/expiring/expired status, coverage types held, and umbrella policy details.

Use this for compliance audits, subcontractor qualification reviews, or to share with your insurance broker.
articles.starRatingsContent:
Project Managers and Admins can rate contractors with a 1–5 star rating directly on their Directory card.

Star ratings are private — they are only visible to users with the PM or Admin role. Contractors, Stakeholders, and Viewers cannot see ratings.

Use star ratings to track contractor performance, reliability, and quality of work internally. Ratings are displayed on the contact card and can help you decide which subcontractors to invite to future projects.
articles.directorySearchContent:
The Staff Directory includes a search bar and type filter buttons to help you find contacts quickly.

Search: Type a name, company, or email in the search bar to filter the directory in real time.

Filter by type: Use the filter buttons (All, Subcontractor, Vendor, Inspector, Employee, Other) to show only contacts of a specific type. The 'All' button resets the filter.

Search and filters work together — you can search within a filtered type, or filter within search results.
articles.staffLocationContent:
Each contact in the Staff Directory can have a Location/City field.

The location is displayed on the contact card below their company name, and is included in CSV exports (both the regular directory export and the compliance export).

Use this field to track where your subcontractors, vendors, and team members are based — helpful for project staffing decisions and travel planning.
articles.jobPLContent:
The Reports page includes a Job P&L (Profit & Loss) table for Project Managers and Admins.

The table shows one row per project with columns for: Budget, Estimated Cost, Actual Cost, Change Orders, Gross Profit, and Margin percentage.

Margin badges are color-coded:
• Green — healthy margin (above 10%)
• Amber — tight margin (0–10%)
• Red — negative margin (losing money)

Use this report to monitor profitability across all your projects at a glance.
articles.voiceNotesContent: The Voice Notes feature lets you record audio directly in the app. Tap the Record button on any phase detail page to start recording (max 5 minutes). Notes are saved automatically and can be played back by any team member. AI transcription is coming soon to make voice notes searchable. You need to grant microphone permission in your browser the first time you use this feature.
articles.rfiBody: Track Requests for Information (RFIs) with numbered sequencing, ball-in-court tracking, priority levels, and response workflows. Create RFIs with questions, assign responsible parties, set due dates, and manage the full lifecycle from open to answered to closed.
articles.submittalBody: Manage the submittal review process with spec section references, revision tracking, and a full approval workflow. Submittals progress through Pending → Under Review → Approved/Approved as Noted/Revise & Resubmit/Rejected. Track revision history and return dates.
articles.timeTrackingBody: Log labor hours for each phase with cost codes, worker assignments, and an approval workflow. Managers can approve or reject entries. View total, approved, and pending hours at a glance. Filter by status to focus on entries needing attention.
articles.keeneyModeContent:
Keeney Mode transforms AccuDone into a hands-free, voice-first experience designed for the jobsite. Named after the voice-driven interface concept, it lets you manage your projects entirely by voice.

Key capabilities:
• Voice commands for creating daily logs, adding photos, updating checklists
• Hands-free navigation between projects and phases
• Voice-to-text for comments and notes with AI transcription
• Audio status updates that are automatically transcribed and logged

To activate Keeney Mode:
1. Open Settings from the bottom navigation
2. Tap "Keeney Mode" under Interface preferences
3. Grant microphone permissions when prompted
4. Use the floating mic button to issue voice commands

Keeney Mode works best in combination with the GPS Time Clock for a fully hands-free field experience.
articles.colorSchemeContent:
Personalize your AccuDone interface with custom color schemes. The theme system lets you choose from preset color palettes or define your own brand colors.

Available options:
• Light and Dark mode base themes
• 8 preset color palettes (Blue, Green, Orange, Purple, Red, Teal, Amber, Indigo)
• Custom primary and accent color picker
• Per-project color coding for quick visual identification

To change your color scheme:
1. Go to Settings > Appearance
2. Select a base theme (Light/Dark)
3. Choose a preset palette or tap "Custom" to pick your own colors
4. Changes apply instantly across the entire app

Your color preference is synced across all your devices.
articles.contactsImportContent:
Quickly build your team directory by importing contacts from external sources. The Contacts Import feature supports CSV files and common contact formats.

Supported import formats:
• CSV files with name, email, phone, and role columns
• vCard (.vcf) files from your phone contacts
• Copy-paste from spreadsheets

To import contacts:
1. Navigate to the Team directory
2. Click "Import Contacts" in the top toolbar
3. Upload your CSV/vCard file or paste from clipboard
4. Map columns to contact fields (name, email, phone, role, company)
5. Review the preview and confirm import

Duplicate detection automatically flags contacts that already exist in your directory. You can choose to skip, merge, or overwrite duplicates during import.
articles.aiDocIntelligenceContent:
AI Document Intelligence automatically analyzes uploaded construction documents to extract key information, identify risks, and suggest actions.

What it analyzes:
• Contracts and subcontracts — extracts key dates, amounts, scope, and obligations
• Change orders — identifies cost impacts and schedule implications
• Submittals and shop drawings — checks for specification compliance
• Insurance certificates — validates coverage and expiration dates
• Lien waivers — verifies completeness and proper execution

How to use it:
1. Upload any document to a project phase
2. AI analysis runs automatically in the background
3. Review the AI summary panel that appears below the document
4. Key findings are highlighted with risk levels (Low/Medium/High)
5. Suggested actions can be converted to checklist items with one tap

AI Document Intelligence works with PDF, Word, and image files. For scanned documents, OCR is applied automatically before analysis.
articles.aiAssistantContent:
The AI Project Assistant is your intelligent co-pilot for construction project management. It can answer questions about your projects, generate reports, draft communications, and provide recommendations.

Capabilities:
• Answer questions about project status, budget, and schedule
• Generate project summary reports and status updates
• Draft RFIs, submittals, and change order descriptions
• Analyze budget trends and flag potential overruns
• Recommend checklist items based on phase type
• Compare progress across multiple projects

To use the AI Assistant:
1. Tap the AI Assistant icon (sparkle icon) from any project page
2. Type or speak your question in natural language
3. The assistant responds with data-driven answers from your project
4. Follow-up questions maintain context from the conversation

The AI Assistant only accesses data from projects you have permission to view. All conversations are private to your account.
articles.aiProactiveAlertsContent:
AI Proactive Alerts monitors your projects 24/7 and automatically flags issues before they become problems. The system analyzes patterns across your project data to generate timely warnings.

Alert categories:
• Budget alerts — spending pace exceeding projections
• Schedule alerts — phases at risk of missing deadlines
• Compliance alerts — expiring insurance certificates, missing documents
• Quality alerts — checklist completion rates below threshold
• Safety alerts — inspection failures or missing safety documentation

Alerts appear in:
• The Attention Required panel on your dashboard
• Push notifications (if enabled)
• The AI Assistant conversation
• Weekly project digest emails

You can customize alert thresholds in Settings > Notifications > AI Alerts. Each alert includes a recommended action that can be executed with one tap.
articles.aiPunchListContent:
AI Punch List Analysis helps you manage and prioritize punch list items more effectively using intelligent categorization and progress tracking.

Features:
• Automatic categorization of punch list items by trade (electrical, plumbing, drywall, etc.)
• Priority scoring based on impact, visibility, and blocking dependencies
• Photo-based defect detection — upload a photo and AI identifies the issue type
• Progress tracking with estimated completion timelines
• Bulk operations for assigning, re-prioritizing, or closing items

To use AI Punch List:
1. Open any project phase and navigate to the Punch List tab
2. Add items manually or use "AI Scan" to detect issues from photos
3. AI automatically categorizes and prioritizes each item
4. Use the "AI Summary" button to generate a punch list report
5. Export the report as PDF for contractor distribution

Bulk Punch List operations let you select multiple items and apply batch actions (assign, change status, set priority) in one step.
articles.aiTimeTrackingContent:
AI Time Tracking Intelligence enhances workforce management by analyzing time entry patterns and providing insights on labor productivity.

Capabilities:
• Automatic overtime detection and alerts
• Labor cost analysis by phase, trade, and worker
• Productivity trend reports with benchmarks
• Anomaly detection for unusual time entries
• Forecasting labor needs based on project schedule

The system works with manual time entries and GPS Time Clock data to provide comprehensive workforce analytics.

To view AI time insights:
1. Navigate to a project's Time Tracking section
2. Click "AI Insights" in the toolbar
3. Review the dashboard showing labor metrics, trends, and alerts
4. Drill down into specific workers, phases, or date ranges

AI recommendations include staffing adjustments, overtime management suggestions, and cost-saving opportunities based on historical patterns.
articles.gpsTimeClockContent:
The GPS Time Clock lets field workers clock in and out with location verification, ensuring accurate time tracking tied to the actual jobsite.

How it works:
• Workers tap "Clock In" when arriving at the jobsite
• GPS coordinates are captured and verified against the project location
• Geofence radius (configurable: 100m to 1km) validates proximity
• Clock-out records departure time and total hours
• Break tracking for compliance with labor regulations

Setup for project managers:
1. Open Project Settings > Time Clock
2. Set the jobsite location on the map (or use project address)
3. Configure the geofence radius
4. Enable/disable break tracking and overtime rules
5. Set which roles are required to use GPS clock-in

For field workers:
1. Open the project from the mobile app
2. Tap the clock icon in the bottom toolbar
3. Tap "Clock In" — your location is verified automatically
4. Tap "Clock Out" when leaving the site

All time entries are visible to project managers in real-time. Location data is used only for verification and is not tracked continuously.
articles.bulkPunchListContent:
Bulk Punch List Operations enable you to manage large punch lists efficiently by applying actions to multiple items at once.

Bulk actions available:
• Assign to contractor — select multiple items and assign them all to one contractor
• Change status — mark multiple items as In Progress, Complete, or Verified
• Set priority — batch update priority levels
• Add tags — apply category tags to multiple items
• Export selection — generate a PDF report of selected items only
• Delete — remove multiple items (requires confirmation)

To use bulk operations:
1. Open the Punch List tab in any phase
2. Tap the checkbox icon to enter selection mode
3. Select individual items or use "Select All"
4. Choose a bulk action from the toolbar
5. Confirm the action

Filters work alongside bulk selection — filter by status, trade, or assignee first, then select all filtered items for batch processing.
articles.photoAnnotationsContent:
Photo Annotations let you mark up progress photos and punch list images with arrows, circles, text labels, and measurement notes directly in the app.

Annotation tools:
• Arrow — point to specific areas of concern
• Circle/Rectangle — highlight regions
• Text label — add descriptions and notes
• Measurement — annotate dimensions (auto-scales with reference)
• Freehand draw — sketch details
• Color picker — choose annotation color for clarity

To annotate a photo:
1. Open any photo in the Photos section or Punch List
2. Tap the pencil icon to enter annotation mode
3. Use the toolbar to select annotation tools
4. Draw, type, and mark up the image
5. Tap "Save" to preserve annotations

Annotated photos are saved as a new version — the original is always preserved. Annotations are visible to all team members with access to the project.
articles.aiEstimatingContent:
AI Estimating uses historical project data and industry benchmarks to help you create accurate cost estimates for new projects and phases.

Capabilities:
• Generate preliminary estimates from project scope descriptions
• Compare estimates against similar completed projects
• Break down estimates by CSI division (concrete, steel, electrical, etc.)
• Adjust estimates for regional cost factors and market conditions
• Track estimate accuracy over time to improve future predictions

To create an AI estimate:
1. Open a project and navigate to Budget > Estimates
2. Click "New AI Estimate"
3. Describe the scope of work or select a phase template
4. AI generates a detailed cost breakdown
5. Review, adjust line items, and finalize the estimate

Estimates can be exported as PDF proposals or imported directly into the project budget. The system learns from your actual project costs to improve estimate accuracy over time.
articles.aiRfiContent:
AI RFI Intelligence streamlines the Request for Information process by auto-drafting RFIs, tracking response times, and identifying patterns.

Features:
• Auto-draft RFI descriptions from photos or document references
• Suggest appropriate recipients based on RFI subject and trade
• Track response times with automated follow-up reminders
• Identify recurring RFI themes that may indicate design issues
• Generate RFI logs and summary reports

To use AI RFI:
1. Navigate to a project's RFIs section
2. Click "New RFI" and describe the question or upload a reference photo
3. AI drafts the RFI description and suggests recipients
4. Review, edit if needed, and submit
5. AI monitors for responses and sends reminders

The AI analyzes your RFI history to flag potential design coordination issues and recommend proactive clarifications before they become field problems.
articles.aiSubmittalContent:
AI Submittal Intelligence automates submittal tracking, reviews submissions for specification compliance, and manages the approval workflow.

Features:
• Auto-generate submittal register from specifications
• Check submittals against specification requirements
• Track review status and approval workflows
• Flag overdue submittals that may impact the schedule
• Generate submittal logs for project documentation

To use AI Submittal:
1. Open a project's Submittals section
2. Upload the submittal document (shop drawings, product data, samples)
3. AI cross-references against project specifications
4. Review AI compliance notes and recommendations
5. Route for approval through the configured workflow

The system maintains a complete audit trail of all submittal reviews, revisions, and approvals for project closeout documentation.
articles.aiChangeOrderContent:
AI Change Order Intelligence analyzes change order requests to assess cost and schedule impacts, identify trends, and recommend negotiation strategies.

Capabilities:
• Analyze change order cost reasonableness against historical data
• Assess schedule impact and critical path implications
• Identify change order trends by cause (design changes, field conditions, owner requests)
• Generate change order summary reports
• Flag potential scope creep patterns

To use AI Change Order analysis:
1. Navigate to the Change Orders section of any project
2. Create or open a change order
3. Click "AI Analysis" to get a cost and impact assessment
4. Review the AI recommendation (approve, negotiate, or reject)
5. Use AI-generated talking points for negotiation

All change orders are tracked against the original contract amount, with AI providing rolling forecasts of total project cost impact.
articles.aiInspectionContent:
AI Inspection Intelligence enhances your inspection workflows with automated checklists, photo-based defect detection, and compliance verification.

Features:
• Auto-generate inspection checklists based on phase type and local codes
• Photo-based defect detection using AI image analysis
• Pass/fail prediction based on historical inspection data
• Automated inspection scheduling tied to project milestones
• Compliance verification against building codes and standards

To use AI Inspection:
1. Open a project phase and go to the Inspections tab
2. Click "Schedule Inspection" or "AI Quick Inspect"
3. For AI Quick Inspect, take photos of the area to be inspected
4. AI analyzes the photos and generates findings
5. Review, approve, and add any manual observations

Inspection results are automatically linked to the project timeline and can trigger follow-up tasks or re-inspection scheduling.
articles.aiMaterialContent:
AI Material Intelligence helps manage material procurement, tracking, and cost optimization across your projects.

Capabilities:
• Generate material takeoffs from project scope and plans
• Track material orders, deliveries, and inventory
• Price comparison across suppliers with historical trending
• Lead time forecasting to prevent schedule delays
• Waste reduction analysis based on historical usage patterns

To use AI Material:
1. Open a project's Materials section
2. Click "AI Material Plan" to generate a procurement schedule
3. Review recommended quantities, suppliers, and lead times
4. Approve purchase orders directly from the material plan
5. Track deliveries against the schedule

The system integrates with your project schedule to ensure materials arrive on time and flags potential supply chain issues before they cause delays.
articles.aiLienWaiverContent:
AI Lien Waiver Intelligence automates the collection, verification, and tracking of lien waivers to protect your projects from liens and payment disputes.

Features:
• Auto-generate lien waiver requests tied to payment applications
• Verify waiver completeness (signatures, notarization, amounts)
• Track waiver status by subcontractor and payment period
• Flag missing or incomplete waivers before payment release
• Generate compliance reports for project closeout

To use AI Lien Waiver:
1. Navigate to the project's Financials > Lien Waivers section
2. AI automatically creates waiver requests when payment apps are submitted
3. Subcontractors receive email notifications to upload signed waivers
4. AI verifies uploaded waivers for completeness and accuracy
5. Dashboard shows waiver collection status at a glance

Conditional and unconditional waivers are tracked separately, with automated progression from conditional to unconditional upon payment confirmation.
articles.aiPaymentAppContent:
AI Payment Application Intelligence streamlines the pay application process with automated schedule-of-values tracking, progress verification, and approval workflows.

Capabilities:
• Auto-calculate current payment based on percent complete
• Verify progress claims against field observations and photos
• Track retainage and release schedules
• Generate AIA G702/G703 style payment applications
• Compare payment requests against budget and schedule

To use AI Payment App:
1. Open the project's Financials > Payment Applications section
2. Click "New Pay App" for the current billing period
3. AI pre-fills progress percentages based on project data
4. Review and adjust line items as needed
5. Route for approval through the configured workflow

The system maintains a complete payment history with audit trail, making project closeout and financial reconciliation straightforward.
articles.aiDrawingContent:
AI Drawing Intelligence provides intelligent management of construction drawings with version control, markup tools, and automated issue detection.

Capabilities:
• Automatic drawing set organization by discipline (architectural, structural, MEP)
• Version comparison with AI-highlighted changes between revisions
• Markup and annotation tools for RFI and issue documentation
• Cross-reference detection between drawing sheets
• OCR-based search across all drawing text and notes

To use AI Drawing:
1. Upload drawing sets to the project's Documents > Drawings section
2. AI automatically organizes by discipline and sheet number
3. Use the comparison tool to highlight changes between revisions
4. Mark up drawings with annotations linked to RFIs or punch list items
5. Search across all drawings by text, detail number, or specification section

Drawing versions are tracked automatically — you can always view any previous revision and see exactly what changed between versions.
articles.predictiveAnalyticsContent:
The Predictive Analytics Dashboard uses AI and historical project data to forecast outcomes and identify risks across your entire portfolio.

Dashboard widgets:
• Project Health Score — overall risk rating combining budget, schedule, and quality metrics
• Budget Forecast — AI-predicted final cost with confidence intervals
• Schedule Forecast — predicted completion dates based on current pace
• Risk Heatmap — visual map of risk factors across all active projects
• Resource Utilization — workforce allocation and capacity forecasting
• Quality Trends — inspection pass rates and punch list velocity

To access Predictive Analytics:
1. Navigate to Analytics from the main dashboard
2. Select the "Predictive" tab
3. Choose a project or view portfolio-wide analytics
4. Drill down into any widget for detailed breakdowns

Forecasts are updated daily based on the latest project data. The system becomes more accurate over time as it learns from your actual project outcomes. All predictions include confidence levels so you can assess reliability.

help.replayIntroDesc: Walk through the app's key features step by step
help.subtitle: Guides, tips, and answers to common questions
help.voiceNotesDesc: Record audio notes directly on any phase for hands-free documentation on the job site.
help.voiceNotesContent: The Voice Notes feature lets you record audio directly in the app. Tap the Record button on any phase detail page to start recording (max 5 minutes). Notes are saved automatically and can be played back by any team member. AI transcription is coming soon to make voice notes searchable. You need to grant microphone permission in your browser the first time you use this feature.
help.punchListBody: Track deficiencies and incomplete work items with the Punch List feature. Create items with priority levels (Critical, Major, Minor, Cosmetic), assign them to team members, set due dates, and track their status through a workflow: Open → In Progress → Ready for Review → Closed. Filter by status to focus on what needs attention. Overdue items are flagged automatically.
help.articles.punchListBody: Track deficiencies and incomplete work items with the Punch List feature. Create items with priority levels (Critical, Major, Minor, Cosmetic), assign them to team members, set due dates, and track their status through a workflow: Open → In Progress → Ready for Review → Closed. Filter by status to focus on what needs attention. Overdue items are flagged automatically.
help.articles.lienWaiversBody: Track conditional and unconditional lien waivers from vendors. Create waivers with type, vendor name, amount, and through-date. Managers can mark waivers as received, approved, or rejected. Approved waivers are automatically flagged as notarized. Filter by status to see pending items needing attention.
help.articles.paymentAppsBody: Create and manage AIA-style payment applications for each phase. Enter period dates, scheduled value, work completed, materials stored, retainage, and previous payments — the system auto-calculates the current amount due. Submit drafts for approval, and managers can approve or reject. Track total due and paid amounts at a glance.
help.articles.drawingsBody: Track all construction drawings organized by discipline (architectural, structural, MEP, civil, etc.). Add drawings with number, title, revision, sheet size, and scale. Manage drawing status — current, superseded, preliminary, for review, or void. Filter by discipline to find drawings quickly. Unique constraint ensures no duplicate drawing number + revision combinations per phase.
help.articles.estimatesBody: Create detailed cost estimates with quantity takeoff line items. Each estimate contains line items with description, quantity, unit, and unit cost — the total is calculated automatically. Organize items by category (labor, material, equipment, etc.). Finalize estimates for review and get manager approval. Track the grand total across all estimates for the phase.
help.articles.adminPanelBody:
The Admin Panel is available exclusively to users with the Admin role. Access it from the sidebar under the Admin link.

Feature Toggles:
Enable or disable platform features globally. Features are grouped by category: Field Operations, Financial, Integrations, and General. Disabled features are hidden from all users.

Activity Log:
View and manage the full system activity log. Admins can undo certain actions directly from the log, including phase status changes, member updates, and checklist toggles.

User Management:
View all users, change roles, and deactivate accounts. Deactivating a user removes them from all projects and ends their active sessions. You cannot change your own role or deactivate yourself.

System Health:
Monitor system statistics including user counts, project counts, document and photo storage, and recent activity metrics.

Audit Export:
Download a CSV file of up to 5,000 recent activity log entries for compliance and record-keeping.
help.articles.plansOverviewBody: AccuDone offers three plans: Starter ($29/mo, 5 users, 10 projects), Pro ($79/mo, 25 users, unlimited projects, AI features, QuickBooks), and Enterprise ($249/mo, unlimited everything, white label, priority support). All plans include a 14-day free trial. Manage your subscription from Settings → Billing. Org owners can upgrade, downgrade, or cancel at any time via the Stripe billing portal.
help.articles.aiUsageBody: AI features (Voice Transcription, Task Extraction, Document AI) require a Pro or Enterprise plan. Each plan includes a monthly token budget (Pro: 100k tokens, Enterprise: 500k tokens). You can monitor usage on the Billing page. When your budget is nearly exhausted (>90%), you'll see a warning. If exceeded, AI features are paused until the next billing cycle. Org owners can request a budget increase from the admin.
help.articles.qbExportBody: Org owners can export payment applications and invoices to QuickBooks directly from the Billing page. First, connect QuickBooks from Settings → QuickBooks. Then use the 'Export to QuickBooks' button on the Billing page. Invoices are marked as exported to prevent duplicates. Sync history is viewable in the QuickBooks settings section.
help.articles.signupOnboardingBody: New users sign up at the /signup page, entering company name, email, password, and selecting a plan. After signup, a 5-step onboarding wizard guides you through: confirming company info, creating your first project, inviting team members, a quick tour of the dashboard, and getting started. All steps except company name are skippable. You can revisit the tour anytime from the Help Center.
help.articles.securityHardeningBody:
AccuDone enforces enterprise-grade security at every layer, with dedicated hardening added in Sprint 36.

IDOR Protection
All API routes verify that you have permission to access the specific resource you are requesting. You cannot access another organization's data by guessing IDs — every query is scoped to your organization.

Rate Limiting
Sensitive endpoints (login, AI calls, exports, report generation) are rate-limited using Redis sliding windows. This prevents brute-force attacks and API abuse.

Content Security Policy (CSP)
Strict CSP headers are served with every response, preventing cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks by controlling which scripts and resources the browser is permitted to load.

Input Validation
All user inputs are validated and sanitized server-side before processing or storage. Malformed or oversized inputs are rejected at the API boundary.

Multi-Tenant Data Isolation
Every database query is scoped to your organization ID. Data from other organizations is never returned in any response, regardless of the request parameters.

These security controls are active on all plans at no additional cost. They require no configuration.
help.articles.aiOverviewBody:
AccuDone includes a comprehensive suite of AI features powered by OpenAI GPT-4o. AI capabilities are available on Pro and Enterprise plans (and during your trial).

AI Feature Areas:
• AI Estimating — Scope-to-estimate generation, completeness review, and historical cost benchmarking
• AI Document Intelligence — PDF parsing, auto-classification, COI auto-fill, and conflict detection
• AI Change Order & Punch List Analysis — Pattern detection and anomaly alerts
• AI RFI & Submittal Drafting — First-draft responses from project context
• AI Inspection Reports — Auto-generated language from inspection data
• AI Time Tracking Intelligence — Labor cost analysis and anomaly detection
• AI Predictive Analytics — Schedule risk scoring, budget forecasting, weather impact
• AI Assistant — Chat interface with tool calling and action execution

AI token usage is tracked per organization. Admins can set monthly token budgets and view usage history in the Admin Panel under AI Usage.
help.articles.aiEstimatingHelpBody:
The AI estimating suite includes three capabilities available from the Estimates section of any phase.

1. Scope-to-Estimate
Paste a text description of the work scope. AI generates a structured estimate with line items covering labor, materials, equipment, and subcontractor costs — organized by CSI division with quantity, unit, unit cost, and line total for each item.

Tip: Be specific — include project type, region, materials specified, and any unusual site conditions for the most accurate output.

2. AI Estimate Review
After creating or importing an estimate, request an AI review. The AI checks for missing line items, flags unusual unit costs compared to typical ranges, and suggests additions based on your scope description.

3. Historical Cost Comparison
AI compares your estimate against similar completed phases in your organization's project history. Significant deviations are flagged with context — useful for catching pricing errors before a bid goes out.
help.articles.aiProjectIntelBody:
AI Project Intelligence covers predictive and pattern-detection features across your projects.

Schedule Risk Scoring
Each phase is scored for schedule risk based on duration variance against historical baselines for similar work types. High-risk phases are surfaced in the dashboard Attention Panel.

Budget Trend Forecasting
S-curve analysis projects your final cost at completion (EAC) with confidence intervals. Forecasts update as actuals are entered.

Weather Impact Analysis
For outdoor phases, AI analyzes the 10-day weather forecast and flags phases at risk of weather-related delays based on activity type and conditions.

Change Order Pattern Detection
AI compares your project's change order frequency and cost impact against similar historical projects. Anomalies trigger alerts before they become budget problems.

Proactive Alerts
The system automatically surfaces alerts for insurance expiry, schedule risk threshold crossings, and budget forecast deviations. These appear in the Attention Panel and as in-app notifications.
help.articles.aiAssistantHelpBody:
The AI Assistant is a chat-based project assistant accessible from the main navigation (the sparkle icon). It can answer questions, generate content, and take actions on your behalf.

What You Can Ask:
• 'What is the budget status on the Riverside project?'
• 'Which of my projects are at highest schedule risk this week?'
• 'Draft a weekly update email for the Downtown Office project'
• 'Create a safety inspection for Phase 3 of the Harbor project'
• 'Summarize the open RFIs across all active projects'

The assistant queries live project data — it does not rely on memory. It has access to your projects, phases, team, documents, budget, activity history, and AI features.

Action Execution
When the assistant takes an action (creates an inspection, sends an update, etc.), it asks for confirmation first. All executed actions are logged in the audit trail and visible in the Admin Panel under Activity Logs.

Tip: The assistant works best with specific, context-rich questions. Mention the project name, phase, or date range for more precise answers.

tour.welcomeDesc: Your all-in-one platform for managing construction projects. Let's take a quick tour of the key features.
tour.welcomeTip: This tour takes about 1 minute. You can skip it anytime.
tour.projectsDesc: Create projects, set budgets, and break work into phases. The Timeline view gives you a Gantt chart showing how everything connects.
tour.projectsTip: Start by creating your first project from the Projects page.
tour.phasesDesc: Each phase tracks status, costs, checklists, documents, and photos. Phases flow from Pending → In Progress → Review → Complete.
tour.phasesTip: Use dependencies to link phases that must happen in sequence.
tour.teamDesc: Invite team members via email with specific roles. Contractors get their own simplified portal for uploading work and requesting reviews.
tour.teamTip: Invite your first team member from any project's Team section.
tour.checklistsDesc: Create reusable checklist templates in Settings and apply them to phases. Upload documents for approval tracking and photos for progress documentation.
tour.checklistsTip: Check Settings > Checklist Templates to set up your first template.
tour.notificationsDesc: Get notified about phase changes, review requests, and completed checklists via in-app, email, or SMS. Customize your preferences in Settings.
tour.notificationsTip: Set up quiet hours to avoid late-night notifications.
tour.contractorWelcomeDesc: This is your Contractor Portal — a focused view of the phases you're assigned to. Let's walk through the basics.
tour.assignedDesc: Your dashboard shows all phases you're assigned to, grouped by status. Click any phase to see its details, checklist, and documents.
tour.assignedTip: Complete checklist items as you finish each task.
tour.uploadDesc: Document your progress by uploading photos and relevant documents to each phase. This creates a clear record for your team.
tour.uploadTip: Take photos regularly — they help during inspections and reviews.
tour.reviewDesc: When you've completed work on a phase, click 'Request Review' to notify the project manager. They'll review and either approve or ask for changes.
tour.reviewTip: Make sure all checklist items are done before requesting a review.
tour.changeOrdersDesc: Track scope changes with formal change orders. When approved, costs automatically roll into your project budget — no manual recalculation needed.
tour.changeOrdersTip: Each change order shows its cost impact before you approve it.
tour.analyticsDesc: Your dashboard shows real-time analytics: budget health, phase progress, and earned value forecasting with CPI metrics to predict if you'll finish under or over budget.
tour.analyticsTip: Check the Budget section for CPI and EAC projections on any project.
tour.offlineDesc: No signal on the job site? No problem. All your changes queue up automatically and sync when you're back online. A status indicator shows pending updates.
tour.offlineTip: Look for the sync indicator in the top bar when you're back online.
tour.offlineContractorDesc: Working without signal? Your uploads, checklist updates, and review requests are saved locally and sync automatically when connectivity returns.
tour.advancedDesc: Share read-only project views with clients via secure token links. Connect your workflow with API webhooks, bulk CSV imports, GPS-tagged photos, and scheduled reports.
tour.advancedTip: Generate a client portal link from any project's Settings tab.
tour.learnMore: Learn more in the Help Center →
